# UC-10.18.3 · Broadcom Symantec SEP Tamper Protection Disable and Bypass Events

## Description

Tamper Protection prevents users or malware from shutting down SEP services. Alerts on disable or bypass attempts are strong indicators of insider misuse, compromised admin credentials, or malware trying to blind the agent.

## Value

Preserves integrity of endpoint controls and accelerates incident response when someone tries to weaken Symantec Endpoint Protection on critical systems.

## Implementation

(1) Forward SEP security / system events to Splunk with TA field extractions. (2) Tune strings for your SEPM locale. (3) Correlate with privileged OS logons on the same host. (4) Map to change tickets for expected maintenance. (5) Escalate uncorrelated events to IR.

## SPL

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-24h
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, Message, message, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)tamper|self.protect|protection disabled|disable tamper|bypass")
| eval user=coalesce(User_Name, user_name, "")
| stats count values(desc) as samples by host user
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (host, user, count, sample text), timeline, single-value (events last 24h).

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2772)
